commit_scope: Extend the native presence beyond a toy affine recipe to a LEARNED-MODEL HTTP service assembled from independently-shared parts: a model-backed handler (the ML part, weights as data via affine-train SGD), a composable middleware layer (the 'anyone can add a layer' part http-server lacked), and an assembly composing them with the data-driven router. Prove the model trains on one process, is shared over a channel as a recipe node, served byte-identically across Go and Rust, and served live by a running presence that also picks up a second contributor's model — no restart.
evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/model-service.fk -> ms-predict (learned model as data), ms-predict-handler (factory closure bound to a presence's weights), ms-body (JSON render)
form/form-stdlib/http-layer.fk -> layer-wrap/layer-stamp/layer-gate/layer-stack: the composable middleware http-server lacked; anyone adds a layer by appending a row
form/form-stdlib/presence-service.fk -> psvc-serve composes router + model handler + chosen layers; model and layers are parameters

Git Bash + Windows toolchain: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/affine-train.fk form-stdlib/model-service.fk form-stdlib/tests/model-serve-core-band.fk -> 15; fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way; 1 ok, 0 divergent
Git Bash + Windows toolchain: cd form && ./validate.sh ... model-service-band.fk -> 255; 1 ok, 0 divergent (three-way; model-capturing + middleware closures)

Git Bash + Windows toolchain: cd form && ./validate.sh ... presence-service-band.fk -> 127; 1 ok, 0 divergent (three-way; full HTTP wire: prediction, model-as-data swap, stamp, gate 401/serve, second route, oracle)

Fourth-arm wall named honestly: model-service fourth=7, presence-service fourth=64 — factory closures capturing runtime data do not cross the emitted walker; only the data-level inference+render core (model-serve-core, verdict 15) is four-way

powershell -File scripts/federation_service.ps1 -> status pass: train_share served {prediction:5964} (learned y=2x, not constant); sibling_parity go==rust=={prediction:5964} identical; live_serve pid_stable served modelA 5964 then contributor C y=3x model 8965, no_restart true, distinct_models true

form/native/presence/form_presence_host.c -> added a kernel-walk (cmd/popen) job kind so a running presence integrates RICH recipes (HTTP/ML) the native lane cannot lower yet; the native dll path is unchanged

fourth_arm_outputs: model-serve-core fks lane: fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables); 1 ok, 0 divergent; verdict 15
model-service / presence-service: three-way only (Go/Rust/TS); fourth-arm wall = factory closures capturing runtime data; NOT listed in fourth-arm-bands.txt

ci_validation.notes: PR CI has not run yet for this branch.

deploy_validation.notes: Local runtime feature. No TCP socket is bound (the verb logic is string->string); the socket carrier is the named host boundary. mac/android live serving reuses proven lowering lanes, not yet run live.

phase_gate.reason: Local four-way + three-way bands and the live federation passed and were adversarially re-verified; CI, deploy, real-socket carrier, and mac/android live remain pending.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The native presence now serves a learned-model HTTP capability assembled from independently-shareable parts. A trained model travels as a content-addressed recipe node and serves identically across kernels; a running presence integrates rich (kernel-walked) recipes and new contributors' models live without restart. Middleware layers compose over any handler.
public_endpoints: local-only: Form stdlib recipes, a kernel-walk presence host, and a PowerShell federation proof; no public HTTP endpoint and no bound socket
test_flows: Four-way core: model-serve-core band -> 15 (Go/Rust/TS/fkwu, 0 divergent) proves the learned inference-as-data and JSON render are portable.

Three-way service: model-service band -> 255 proves the model-backed handler closure and the stamp/gate middleware at the value level; presence-service band -> 127 proves the full HTTP wire including model-as-data swap, the stamp header, the gate 401-without/serve-with, and a second added route.
Federation share: a model trained by SGD on process A is frozen to a channel and read by process B; Go and Rust serve the byte-identical learned prediction (content-addressed).

Live integration: a running presence serves the shared model via a kernel-walk job, then serves a second contributor's differently-trained model, at a stable PID with one awake event — a new part integrated with no restart.
Honest wall: the closure composition is three-way; the fourth-arm capacity wall (closures capturing runtime data) is named, not papered over.
